name: Why Nogales Fails - Econlib
abstract: This article critically re-examines Daron Acemoglu and James A. Robinson's famous 'Nogales vs. Nogales' example from "Why Nations Fail," which posits that institutional differences explain wealth disparities between Nogales, Arizona, and Nogales, Sonora. The author argues that while institutions are crucial, the Nogales case actually demonstrates the profound economic benefits of open trade integration, particularly through NAFTA/USMCA. Contrary to being a stark contrast of inclusive versus extractive institutions, the article shows how cross-border trade has led to significant manufacturing expansion, job creation, and strategic logistics hubs on both sides, effectively 'importing' inclusive institutional benefits south of the border. It highlights the deeply intertwined nature of the two cities and the substantial trade volumes, suggesting the case is a powerful testament to open trade's long-term advantages, despite existing income disparities.
